results.title: Alleato Group Team Intro
results.summary:
👥 Team Introductions: Tim Stone joins as CFO with construction experience, ensuring reliable financials for Alito Aliado and WIP understanding.
⚙️ Accounting Improvements: Standardized systems and automation prioritized to enhance accuracy and speed in WIP reporting and invoicing.
🔍 Billing Transparency: Issues with unexplained bills addressed; approval limits adjusted to enforce clarity and accountability on charges.
📜 Licensing Support: Tim to assist with contractor licensing across 22 states, aiming to streamline processes and reduce administrative delays.
📈 Financial Reporting Goals: Emphasis on timely financials and cash flow statements to support strategic planning discussions for 2026.
🔄 Transition Planning: Fatima ensures seamless handoff by maintaining access and coordinating

results.title: Review session
results.summary:
💵 $2 million billing mismatch: Identified mismatch between Planner and Akumatica, with missing payments of $88,000 and $10,000.
✅ $5.4 million total payments for iBeach applied: Resolved balances, including a $500,000 deposit and a $2.17 million payment.
📉 $448,455.71 outstanding for Craving Crepes: Recorded after recent payments; clear payment trails improved accuracy significantly.
🗓️ Payments must align with invoice periods: Standardizing this process will minimize future workload.
🔄 Job Planner to be updated: Ensuring alignment with Akumatica for reporting and audit readiness.
📊 Unified spreadsheets for invoices and payments: Implemented for quick identification of gaps and ensuring accuracy in reconciliation.

results.title: Paradise Isles
results.summary:
📅 Biweekly Meetings Update: Shift to structured updates across six project categories to enhance project clarity and coordination.
🏗️ Union Project Progress: Contract finalization nearing with a budget of approximately $8 million; funding targeted by May 1st.
🏙️ Port Project Status: City expected to finalize property slivers by mid-February, moving project forward upon approval.
💰 Kansas City Incentives: Identified key incentives: TIF, CID, and industrial bond; potential design start pushed beyond winter due to approval timelines.
🤝 Rufit Leadership Dispute: Ongoing compensation issues with Russ; decision on his role and pay structure due within 24 hours.
🔄 Operational Improvements: Emphasis on better communication and engagement, including more responsive contact strategies and care

results.title: Accounting organization & tasks lists
results.summary:
📊 Job Planner and Acumatica Integration: Jobs sync with Acumatica for accurate billing; expenses are logged and tracked through Notion for budgeting.
💰 Cost Categories Clarified: Committed costs require vendor approval; direct costs use Notion for accountability, ensuring accurate coding by project managers.
🗓️ Improved Invoice Tracking: New fields for submission dates in Notion proposed to prevent late payments and streamline invoice processing.
📝 Vendor Setup Procedures: Only authorized accounting staff can create vendors; W9 forms and COIs are required for setup to prevent duplicates.
💳 Payment Processing Overview: Direct costs paid on net 30 terms; committed costs follow owner payment receipt. Issues with retainage and contract accuracy addressed.
📈 Cash Flow Reporting Needs: Cur

results.title: GPC9406 / High bay pallet racking
results.summary:
**Executive Summary of Meeting GPC9406: High Bay Pallet Racking**

**Meeting Purpose and Key Participants:**
The meeting GPC9406, held on October 10, 2023, aimed to discuss the implementation and optimization of high bay pallet racking systems within the warehouse operations. Key participants included John Smith, Operations Manager; Sarah Johnson, Warehouse Supervisor; and Mark Lee, Logistics Coordinator. The meeting was convened to address current challenges in inventory management and to explore solutions that could enhance storage efficiency and space utilization.

**Main Topics Discussed:**
The discussion centered around the current state of the warehouse layout and the limitations posed by existing racking systems. John Smith presented data indicating that the current racking configur
results.actionItems:
*Brandon Clymer**
Verify NFPA standards and determine if alternative clearance tables can apply to allow 15 ft clearance, potentially reducing current constraints (16:33)
Communicate findings on applicable fire protection standards and clarify risks and options with alleatogroup and other stakeholders (16:33)
Potentially contact Bill Key for further clarifications as needed during the standard review process (16:33)
results.participants: (mohammed.radwan@hy-tek.com), (bclymer@alleatogroup.com), (awehner@alleatogroup.com), (bill.key@hy-tek.com), Meeting initiated without Alec present., 📏 **Pallet Rack Heights and Sprinkler System Clearance** (01:08 - 02:50), Clarification sought on pallet rack heights and sprinkler clearance due to discrepancies in measurements., Bill explained stacking height calculations leading to total clearance of 170-172 inches., 📦 **Stored Products Discussion** (03:26 - 04:26), Discussion on types of stored products, primarily automotive-related, including some combustible and hazardous materials., Hazardous materials stored separately in a hazmat room., 📊 **Racking Drawings Review** (04:26 - 07:33), Brandon shared racking drawings and sprinkler system layout, confirming rack heights and layouts., Layouts correspond to Bill's clearance figures., ✅ **Pallet Height Confirmation** (07:33 - 09:28), Confirmation that pallets are maximum 72 inches high with 6 inches lift off needed for sprinkler head clearance., Total clearance achieved by stacking and beam spacing., 📐 **Beam Thickness and Clearance** (09:30 - 09:58), Confirmed beam thickness as 4 inches based on drawings, affecting clearance., Discussion on how this impacts overall design., 🔍 **FM Global Standards Discussion** (10:37 - 12:40), Clarified adherence to FM Global standards; facility is self-insured and not strictly required to follow FM Global., Aiming for safety standards while adhering to applicable codes., 📝 **Fire Marshal Approval** (12:42 - 13:37), Fire marshal approved drawings with potential caveats; no issues from official inspection., Internal questions about compliance standards raised., 📅 **Prior Discussions Recap** (13:37 - 16:32), Bill recalled discussions from July about maintaining pallet heights influencing sprinkler design., Design adjustments included upgrading rack uprights for compliance., 🔄 **NFPA Standards Review** (16:33 - 18:41), Brandon indicated need to double-check NFPA standards for alternative clearance options., Acknowledged internal miscommunication on standard application., ⚠️ **Additional Beams Impact** (18:42 - 19:02), Noted that additional beams added to protect sprinkler heads increased overall rack height., Contributed to clearance requirements., 📞 **Communication Commitment** (19:02 - 19:34), Brandon admitted shortcomings in communicating code requirements., Committed to investigating options and providing updates to Bill and Mohammed., Mohammed Radwan, Brandon Clymer, Bill Key

provider_decision.reason: Streaming model tools enabled on the configured AI SDK provider. Vercel AI Gateway is preferred when AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is configured; direct OpenAI requires AI_PROVIDER_PATH=openai or no gateway key. Disable by setting AI_ASSISTANT_DISABLE_STREAMING_MODEL_TOOLS=true.

steps.label: Intent and project scope
steps.whenUsed: Every turn. The assistant classifies the request, applies selected project scope, and decides whether the answer needs packet, structured, source, or write-action handling.
steps.failureMode: If the request is ambiguous, AA should ask for the missing project or action detail instead of guessing.
steps.sourceOfTruth: intent router, selectedProjectId, project access guardrails

steps.label: Current intelligence packet
steps.whenUsed: Project status, risk, catch-up, owner-readout, and strategic questions use the current packet first when a fresh packet exists.
steps.failureMode: If the packet is missing or stale, AA must say that and continue with structured/source retrieval.

steps.label: Project operating snapshot
steps.whenUsed: Used when the question needs structured current-state data such as project controls, tasks, financials, and operating-summary source coverage.
steps.failureMode: If a source category is unavailable, AA should surface the gap rather than treating the snapshot as complete.
steps.sourceOfTruth: project briefing snapshot and operating-summary services

steps.label: Structured tools
steps.whenUsed: Used for precise totals, counts, IDs, statuses, ownership, and workflow records.
steps.failureMode: If a query fails, AA should return the tool-error envelope and explain which source failed.
steps.sourceOfTruth: Supabase tables/views and scoped project tools

steps.label: Source-specific retrieval
steps.whenUsed: Used for meetings, emails, Teams messages, documents, app help, and questions where the answer lives in unstructured communications.
steps.failureMode: If a source is empty or timed out, AA should cite the attempted source and answer from remaining evidence only.

steps.label: Specialist or action tools
steps.whenUsed: Used after evidence is scoped when a CFO/COO/CRO/CHRO/VPBD/CMO opinion or preview-first write workflow is needed.
steps.failureMode: Writes must stay preview-first unless the tool has explicit confirmation and idempotency protection.
steps.sourceOfTruth: C-suite orchestrator, action tools, feature request tools

steps.label: Answer, citations, and trace
steps.whenUsed: Every turn. AA persists tool traces, provider decision, loop diagnostic, source health, quality score, and retrieval policy metadata to chat history.
steps.failureMode: If metadata cannot be persisted, the request should fail loudly instead of presenting observability as complete.

assistant_retrieval_order.summary: AA scopes the question first, prefers fresh compiled project intelligence for strategic questions, layers structured project data, then uses source-specific retrieval and specialist/action tools as needed.
